detailed_analysis: claude_io.py is a small, focused Layer-3 interface adapter (152 lines, 3 functions, 0 classes) implementing the Claude half of the CustomizationTypeIO port: parse_claude_md, render_claude_md, plus a private _split_csv helper. It correctly delegates all shared Markdown/YAML metadata-block primitives to markdown_yaml_metadata_block (split_frontmatter, frontmatter_for_render, yaml_dump, extract_pair_id_from_md), so it is not a duplicate of the other adapters. It honours the Dependency Rule (imports only from canonical, field_names, and the shared metadata-block module — all same-layer or inward). No security smells: no secrets, no injection vectors, no shell/eval, no unsafe deserialization (YAML loading is delegated to the bounded round-trip loader). It correctly does NOT evaluate secret_policy itself — per architecture Goal 6 / NFR-15 that is the sync core's responsibility, and Claude MCP-server secret handling lives in a separate mcp_server_io path. The file is well under the 300-line module limit and both public functions sit at/below the 40-line function limit. The defects that exist are maintainability-grade: parse_claude_md is exactly at the 40-line boundary and mixes several field-mapping responsibilities as a flat sequence of if-blocks (a mild SRP/DRY pressure — six near-identical 'if X in frontmatter -> assign' blocks plus two near-identical list-or-csv coercions); a partial reliance on named field constants (parse_claude_md uses raw string literals 'name'/'description'/'model'/'effort'/'tools'/'pair_id' on the parse side while the symmetric render side and the disallowed/permission/hooks/mcp paths use the CanonicalField/ClaudeField constants — an internal naming inconsistency that the field_names module exists specifically to remove); and missing type precision on the dict-returning public functions (dict[str, Any] is a primitive-obsession schema-by-convention, but that is the project-wide canonical representation, not a defect local to this file). No CRITICAL or MAJOR findings. Spec/architecture conformance is clean against the adapter contract in architecture §5.1, US-10, and the Goal-6 egress invariant.

findings.problem: The parse path uses raw string literals ('name', 'description', 'model', 'effort', 'tools', 'pair_id') for canonical field keys, while the render path (lines 122-136) and the disallowed/permission/hooks/mcp branches use the CanonicalField.* / ClaudeField.* constants from field_names.py. field_names.py was introduced (per its docstring, 'audit slice 07 / CQ-15') specifically to anchor these keys to named constants so a reader does not chase literals across modules. The same module mixing both conventions is the exact inconsistency that module is meant to eliminate. Not a correctness bug — the literals match the constant values — but a documented project anti-pattern (No-Hardcoding / one-concept-one-word).

findings.evidence:
def parse_claude_md(text, prior_canonical=None, *, kind='agent') -> dict[str, Any]:
    ... 40 lines, ~11 branches (one per optional frontmatter field plus two list-or-csv ternaries) ...
findings.problem: parse_claude_md is exactly at the project's 40-line function limit (counting body) and just over the cyclomatic-complexity threshold of 10 (one branch per optional field plus the raw_tools / raw_dis isinstance ternaries). It is a flat field-by-field translation, so cognitive load per branch is low, but it carries several distinct mapping concerns (scalar fields, list/CSV fields, the per_agentic_tool_only bag, the per_agentic_tool_extra bag, and pair_id seeding) in one body. It is the most likely function in this file to breach the limit on the next field addition. Borderline rather than a clear violation.

findings.evidence:
raw_tools = frontmatter_data.get("tools")
if raw_tools is not None:
    canonical["tools"] = (list(raw_tools) if isinstance(raw_tools, list) else _split_csv(raw_tools))
... (lines 77-81) raw_dis = frontmatter_data.get(ClaudeField.DISALLOWED_TOOLS) ... same list-or-csv shape
findings.problem: Two structurally identical 'fetch -> if not None -> list(...) if isinstance(list) else _split_csv(...)' blocks differ only in the source key and destination key. as_string_list() in markdown_yaml_metadata_block already implements exactly this list-or-CSV coercion and is part of the shared public API the file is otherwise built on, yet this file re-derives the pattern inline with the local _split_csv helper instead. Minor duplication that the shared helper was designed to absorb.

_split_csv is a near-exact copy of the str branch of as_string_list (markdown_yaml_metadata_block lines 193-199), which the shared module exposes precisely so adapters stop hand-rolling CSV/list coercion. The local helper also has a single-letter-ish opaque name; 'split_csv' does not say 'coerce a frontmatter scalar-or-csv to a list of strings'. The module-level docstring (lines 4-8) advertises that the shared primitives exist to avoid 'four near-duplicates' — this is a fifth.

findings.evidence:
claude_only = canonical.get(CanonicalField.PER_AGENTIC_TOOL_ONLY, {}).get("claude", {})
... (line 144) for key, value in canonical.get("per_agentic_tool_extra", {}).get("claude", {}).items():
findings.problem: Two chained .get() calls on a dict[str, Any] reach two levels into the canonical's nested passthrough bags with inline default {} at each hop. Because canonical is a plain dict[str, Any] (primitive-obsession schema-by-convention), these chains are unchecked and would silently degrade to empty on a malformed canonical rather than fail loud at the boundary (slight tension with the project's fail-fast guideline). Note line 144 uses the literal 'per_agentic_tool_extra' while line 138 uses CanonicalField.PER_AGENTIC_TOOL_ONLY — same mixed-vocabulary issue as CQ-01. Low severity because the canonical shape is produced internally and empty_canonical seeds these bags.

notes: Architecture Goal 6 / NFR-15 (secret_policy at every egress): NOT a deviation. The architecture explicitly assigns secret-policy evaluation to the sync core, not per adapter (Goal 6 row: 'evaluated by the sync core ... not per adapter'). claude_io correctly performs no secret evaluation; Claude MCP-server secret handling lives in the separate mcp_server_io path.

Architecture §5.1 rule 4 (unknown fields not dropped): SATISFIED. parse_claude_md routes any frontmatter key not in KNOWN_CLAUDE_FIELDS into per_agentic_tool_extra['claude'] (lines 97-103); render replays them (lines 144-145).

Architecture §5.1 rule 5 / NFR-06 (no foreign-tool fields leak): SATISFIED. render only emits canonical-owned fields plus Claude's own per_agentic_tool_only['claude'] and per_agentic_tool_extra['claude'] bags; no other tool's bag is read.
Dependency Rule (architecture §3): SATISFIED. Imports only canonical (Layer 1/3), field_names, and the shared metadata-block module; no use-case, daemon, cli, or config import.

Goal 5 / §1 'sync engine never references a concrete tool name': N/A to this module — it IS the concrete adapter; the literal 'claude' here is expected per §1 ('the names appear in adapter modules ... and nowhere else').

verdict_summary: Clean, well-scoped Claude adapter; no CRITICAL/MAJOR findings; only maintainability smells (mixed field-key vocabulary, duplicated CSV coercion, get-chains). code_score 0.75.
